skill.name: extract
skill.description: Extract and consolidate reusable components, design tokens, and patterns into your design system. Identifies opportunities for systematic reuse and enriches your component library.
skill.summary: Extract reusable UI components, design tokens, and patterns into a maintainable design system.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives: SKILL.md uses design-system terms like components, design tokens, and patterns, not cryptographic functions. The network reconnaissance hit at SKILL.md:71 is also a false positive because it refers to generic UI components, not scanning or network activity. No executable code, prompt injection attempt, data exfiltration, command execution, or real network behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Teams often repeat UI code, colors, spacing, and layout patterns across features. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code identify reusable pieces and consolidate them into a design system.
actual_capabilities: Finds existing design system, component library, or shared UI directories.
Identifies repeated UI components, hard-coded values, and inconsistent variations.
Plans which components, tokens, variants, and names should be extracted.
Guides creation of accessible reusable components with clear props and examples.
Outlines migration steps to replace duplicate implementations with shared versions.
Prompts documentation updates for component catalogs or Storybook.
limitations: It provides workflow guidance and does not include executable automation scripts.
It depends on the host AI tool to inspect and edit the project correctly.
It cannot judge visual quality without project context or rendered output.
It asks for confirmation before creating a new design system.

use_cases.title: Consolidate Repeated UI
use_cases.description: Identify repeated buttons, cards, inputs, and layouts, then move them into shared components.

use_cases.title: Create Design Tokens
use_cases.description: Convert repeated colors, spacing, typography, and shadows into named tokens with clear usage guidance.

use_cases.title: Document Component Patterns
use_cases.description: Add usage notes, examples, variants, and migration guidance for newly extracted reusable patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Find Reuse Opportunities
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a feature area
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the extract skill on this feature area. Find repeated UI patterns, hard-coded values, and components that may belong in the design system.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Component Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the extract skill for this page. Propose which components, variants, props, and tokens should be extracted, and explain the migration path.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Into Shared Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the extract skill to create reusable components from the repeated patterns in this module. Preserve behavior, accessibility, and visual parity.

prompt_templates.title: Enrich the Design System
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the extract skill across these related flows. Extract reusable components, add semantic tokens, update documentation, and replace existing usages systematically.

output_examples.input: A checkout page has three custom button styles and repeated spacing values.
output_examples.output: A recommendation to create shared Button variants, semantic spacing tokens, and a migration list for each current usage.

output_examples.input: A dashboard repeats card layouts with different headers and actions.
output_examples.output: A plan for a reusable card component with documented variants, accessibility notes, and examples for common dashboard patterns.

output_examples.input: A project lacks a visible design system directory.
output_examples.output: A request for confirmation before creating a design system, including suggested locations and structure options.

best_practices: Start with patterns used at least three times or likely to be reused soon.
Match existing component, token, documentation, and export conventions.
Verify visual parity, accessibility behavior, and TypeScript types after migration.
anti_patterns: Do not extract one-off UI that only serves a single context.
Do not create overly generic components without clear usage boundaries.
Do not create tokens for every value without semantic meaning.

faq.question: Does this skill create a design system automatically?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the AI to ask before creating one when no design system exists.

faq.question: Can it work with an existing component library?
faq.answer: Yes. It first looks for existing structure, naming, documentation, and import conventions.

faq.question: Does it include scripts or code generators?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a guidance file for AI-assisted analysis and refactoring.

faq.question: What kinds of tokens does it target?
faq.answer: It targets reusable colors, spacing, typography, shadows, and other values with semantic meaning.

faq.question: How does it decide what to extract?
faq.answer: It weighs reuse frequency, consistency benefits, generality, and maintenance cost.

faq.question: Can it update documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides updates to component documentation, examples, guidelines, and Storybook catalogs.
